description: Routing table for a hermetic OpenGrok REST fake (the httptest backend in references/backend-strategies.md). For each incoming request the fake serves the FIRST route whose `path` matches and whose `match` map (query-param name -> exact value) is fully satisfied. A route with "default": true matches any request to that path. Unmatched /search requests MUST fall through to search_empty.json so the server's startup capability probes (full=test, def=test, refs=test) still get HTTP 200 and keep their capabilities enabled.
notes: Search query params are named by mode: full (full_text), def (definition), refs (reference), path (path), hist (history). The query value is the param's value, e.g. full=Engine.

The server AUTO-QUOTES bare multi-word queries, so a case with query 'final class' arrives as full="final class" (with quotes). Exact-value matching on multi-word queries is therefore brittle — prefer single-token match values, and let multi-word cases fall through to search_empty.json (they still assert no_error).
Project listing and overview both hit /list?path=<project>. /file/content uses path=<project>/<file>.
